Plaintiff Knakalia Bryant???S Motion For Dismissal Of Individual Claims With Prejudice And Class Claims Without Prejudice; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof (Motions)

Atty. ANNA K. WALTHER, Esq. of Crosner Legal, P.C. (Tel: 310-496-5818) for plaintiff;
Atty. PENNY CHEN, Esq. of K&L Gates LLC (Tel: 310-552-5033) for defendant John Stewart Company.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-02-2020 Continued To Mar-17-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Brittany Armstrong, Esq. 707-718-8832 for Plaintiff.
Shahram Samie, Esq. 310-553-0308 of Littler Mendelson for Defendants.

RULING - A R G U E D.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT JACK GARDNER, THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY's MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Defendants John Stewart Company and Jack Gardner's motion to compel arbitration is granted.
"On petition of a party to an arbitration agreement alleging the existence of a written agreement to arbitrate a controversy...the court shall order the petitioner and the respondent to arbitrate the controversy if it determines that an agreement to arbitrate the controversy exists." (CCP 1281.2.)

Plaintiff and defendant John Stewart Company entered into an arbitration agreement in January 2019. The agreement delegates to the arbitrator "disputes arising out of or relating to interpretation or application of this Agreement, including the enforceability, revocability or validity of the Agreement or any portion of the Agreement." (Choi Decl. Ex. C, p. 1.) Plaintiff's opposition makes no "specific challenge to the enforceability of the delegation clause," but rather argues "that the agreement as a whole is unenforceable." (See Malone v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 1551, 1559-60.) The Supreme Court "has consistently held that parties may delegate threshold arbitrability questions to the arbitrator, so long as the parties' agreement does so by 'clear and unmistakable' evidence." (Henry Schein, Inc. v. Archer and White Sales, Inc. (2019) 139 S.Ct. 524, 530.) Plaintiff does not suggest that California arbitration law is to the contrary.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
